questions.q: What kind of creature was Jatayu?
questions.explanation: Jatayu was an aged vulture, a king among birds, who fought to protect Sita.

questions.q: Whom was Jatayu trying to rescue when he fought in the sky?
questions.explanation: Jatayu attacked Ravana to save Sita, who was being carried away.

questions.q: Who was the demon king that Jatayu fought?
questions.explanation: Jatayu battled Ravana, the ten-headed king of Lanka, as he abducted Sita.

questions.q: Where did the battle between Jatayu and Ravana take place?
questions.explanation: Jatayu intercepted Ravana's flying chariot and fought him in the air.

questions.q: What was the outcome of Jatayu's fight with Ravana?
questions.explanation: Though brave, the aged Jatayu was mortally wounded by Ravana in the duel.

questions.q: Before dying, what crucial information did Jatayu give Rama?
questions.explanation: With his last breath Jatayu told Rama the direction in which Sita had been carried.

questions.q: Jatayu's act in fighting Ravana is best described as one of:
questions.explanation: Jatayu gave his life trying to save Sita, an act of selfless sacrifice.

questions.q: Whom did Jatayu inform about Sita's abduction before he died?
questions.explanation: Jatayu lived just long enough to tell Rama what had happened to Sita.

questions.q: In what vehicle was Ravana carrying Sita when Jatayu attacked?
questions.explanation: Ravana abducted Sita in his aerial chariot, which Jatayu confronted in the sky.

questions.q: How is Jatayu generally described in terms of age?
questions.explanation: Jatayu was an aged vulture, which made his bold stand all the more striking.

questions.q: Who was Jatayu's brother, also a great bird of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Jatayu's brother was Sampati, who later helped the search party locate Sita.

questions.q: Jatayu had a friendship with which king of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Jatayu was a friend of King Dasharatha, Rama's father, from an earlier generation.

questions.q: What did Ravana use to ultimately bring down Jatayu in the fight?
options: His sword, severing the bird's wings
questions.explanation: Ravana drew his sword and cut off Jatayu's wings, sending the bird crashing down.

questions.q: Which brother accompanied Rama when they found the dying Jatayu?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana was with Rama during the search and at Jatayu's deathbed.

questions.q: What did Jatayu damage on Ravana's chariot during the battle?
options: The chariot itself, breaking it down
questions.explanation: Jatayu fiercely tore at the chariot, killing its donkeys and breaking it before Ravana recovered.

questions.q: After Jatayu died, what did Rama do for him?
options: Left the body where it lay
questions.explanation: Rama performed the cremation and last rites for Jatayu as he would for a kinsman.

questions.q: In which kanda (book) of Valmiki's Ramayana does Jatayu's fight with Ravana occur?
questions.explanation: The episode takes place in the Aranya Kanda, during the events of the forest exile.

questions.q: Why did Jatayu first urge Ravana to stop before attacking?
options: To warn him against the sin of abducting another's wife
To challenge him to a race
questions.explanation: Jatayu reasoned with Ravana, warning him of the grave sin of carrying off Rama's wife.

questions.q: Jatayu told Rama that Sita's abductor was heading in which direction?
questions.explanation: Jatayu indicated Ravana had taken Sita toward the south, where Lanka lay.

questions.q: What was the broad lineage of Jatayu and his brother as great birds?
options: Born of Garuda's race / sons of Aruna
questions.explanation: Jatayu and Sampati were sons of Aruna and belonged to the noble race of mighty birds.

questions.q: Sita, while being carried away, hoped Jatayu would do what?
options: Fight Ravana and tell Rama of her plight
questions.explanation: Sita called on Jatayu and hoped he would resist Ravana and inform Rama of her abduction.

questions.q: How did Rama initially react when he first saw the wounded Jatayu in the forest?
options: He suspected the bird had eaten Sita
He flew into a rage at Lakshmana
questions.explanation: Rama at first wondered if the blood-covered bird was a demon who had devoured Sita, before learning the truth.

questions.q: In Valmiki's account, what part of Ravana's chariot team did Jatayu destroy?
options: The golden donkeys drawing the chariot
questions.explanation: Jatayu killed the donkeys (with goblin-like faces) that drew Ravana's aerial chariot and shattered it.

questions.q: Jatayu's brother Sampati had earlier suffered what injury, related to the sun?
options: His wings were burned shielding Jatayu from the sun
He lost his beak to a storm
questions.explanation: Sampati's wings were scorched when he shielded the younger Jatayu from the sun's heat in their youthful flight.

questions.q: Which character first encountered Jatayu and mistook him for a demon guarding the forest, before the bird revealed his identity?
questions.explanation: Rama himself, searching for Sita, came upon the fallen Jatayu and briefly took him for a demon.

questions.q: According to tradition, attaining liberation through Rama's hands is the boon credited to Jatayu's:
options: Death in Rama's presence and Rama's performance of his rites
questions.explanation: Jatayu is said to attain a blessed state because he died serving Rama, who performed his funeral rites.

questions.q: Whose son were Jatayu and Sampati, in the lineage of birds?
options: Aruna, the charioteer of the sun
questions.explanation: Jatayu and Sampati were sons of Aruna, the dawn-charioteer of the sun and brother of Garuda.

questions.q: After Jatayu's wings were severed and he fell, what did Ravana do next with Sita?
questions.explanation: Once Jatayu fell, Ravana seized Sita again and resumed his flight toward Lanka.

questions.q: Where, broadly, did Jatayu dwell and keep watch before intervening, in the region of Rama's exile?
options: Near Janasthana in the Dandaka forest
On the slopes of Mount Kailasa
questions.explanation: Jatayu lived in the Dandaka forest near Janasthana, the region where Rama dwelt in exile.

questions.q: What does Jatayu's stand against Ravana most exemplify in the Ramayana's moral framework?
options: The triumph of cunning over force
Dharma upheld even at the cost of one's life
The reward of wealth for loyalty
questions.explanation: Jatayu embodies dharma, choosing to defend the helpless and oppose adharma even unto death.
